# Red Hat Standard — Presentation Skills

> **Agent instruction**: Read this file in full before generating or editing any
> slide in the `red-hat-standard` template. Every rule here is derived directly from the
> official Red Hat template file (April 2026).

---

## 1. Template Identity

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Template ID | `red-hat-standard` |
| Slide size | 13.00" × 7.00" (widescreen 16:9) |
| Primary heading font | Red Hat Display |
| Body font | Red Hat Display |
| Primary brand colour | `#EE0000` (Red Hat Red) |
| Dark accent | `#5F0000` |
| Light background | `#f5f5f5` |

### Full Colour Palette

| Role | Hex |
|------|-----|
| dk1 (text/black) | `#000000` |
| lt1 (white) | `#FFFFFF` |
| dk2 (dark accent) | `#5F0000` |
| lt2 (light accent) | `#A60000` |
| accent1 | `#EE0000` |
| accent2 | `#F56E6E` |
| accent3 | `#F56E6E` |
| accent4 | `#F2F2F2` |
| accent5 | `#63993D` |
| accent6 | `#F0561D` |

---

## 2. Brand Rules (Non-Negotiable)

These rules are extracted verbatim from the official template:

- Updating the confidential designator:
- Copy the appropriate designation into the “Confidential designator” field in the upper right.
- Red Hat associate and NDA partner use only,
- Confidential: Red Hat associate and NDA partner use only, No further distribution
- Use this designation for a confidential presentation intended only for the Red Hat associate(s) and partner(s) with signed NDA who receive the deck originally. The Red Hat and NDA partner associate(s) who receive this deck cannot share it with anyone—even other Red Hat and partner associate(s).
- Red Hat associates only
- Confidential: Red Hat associates only
- Use this designation for a confidential presentation that can only be shared with Red Hat associates. The Red Hat associate(s) who receive this deck can share it with other Red Hat associate(s), but no one else.
- Red Hat associates only, no further distribution
- Confidential: Red Hat associates only, No further distribution
- Use this designation for a confidential presentation intended only for the Red Hat associate(s) who receive it originally. The Red Hat associate(s) who receive this presentation cannot share it with anyone—inside or outside of Red Hat.
- For more information on how to update the confidential designator, learn more

### Core Design Principle
> *"To build brand awareness, stick to the essentials. Use our logos, core colors,
> icons, and typography to create clean, intentional designs that are
> unmistakably Red Hat."*

---

## 3. Deck Sections

Every Red Hat Standard deck is structured around these sections (in order):

1. **Getting started**
2. **Title and closing slides**
3. **Dividers**
4. **Overview, agenda, content, and quotes**
5. **Data, tables, and timelines**

---

## 4. Slide Elements

Each element type has a dedicated skill YAML in `elements/`. Quick reference:

| Element | File | When to use |
|---------|------|-------------|
| Title Block | `elements/title-block.yaml` | First and last slide of every deck |
| Section Divider | `elements/divider.yaml` | Between major sections |
| Agenda / Overview | `elements/agenda.yaml` | Slide 2 or 3 |
| Metric Card | `elements/metric-card.yaml` | KPIs, market stats (max 4/slide) |
| Quote Block | `elements/quote-block.yaml` | Customer or analyst voice |
| Challenge List | `elements/challenge-list.yaml` | Pain point framing |
| Tech Tile Grid | `elements/tech-tile.yaml` | Product/solution portfolio |
| Recommendation Card | `elements/recommendation-card.yaml` | Closing next steps |
| Bar / Column Chart | `elements/bar-chart.yaml` | Category comparison data |
| Data Table | `elements/data-table.yaml` | Feature/spec comparison |
| Timeline | `elements/timeline.yaml` | Roadmap or phased delivery |
| Image + Content | `elements/image-content.yaml` | Story with visual reinforcement |

---

## 5. Layout Quick Tips (from template)

#### Getting started
- Update or remove the project number, event name or hashtag on the theme slide.
- For more information on how to update the confidential designator, learn more

#### Title and closing slides
- If the presentation is not product focused, simply delete this. Do
- insert the Red Hat logo here. If this is a product-focused deck, right click on the logo and using “Replace Image” insert the product logo of your choice. After replacing the image, right click and select “Reset Image”. Adjust spacing as needed.
- Try right clicking on the photo and using “Replace Image” to insert a different photo.

#### Overview, agenda, content, and quotes
- Insert image in this designated area, deleting the shaded background. Keep the left and right margins clear to maintain the open feel in accordance with the brand.
- If no subheading is needed, delete the subheading text, and the content can extend into the lighter shaded area.
- Insert image in this designated area, deleting the shaded background. Keep the left, right, top, and bottom margins clear to maintain the open feel in accordance with the brand.
- Try right clicking on the photo and using “Replace Image” to insert a different photo. You are also welcome to use this photo.
- To edit a chart, select it and click the dropdown arrow in the top right. Select “Open source.” The percentage will need to be updated manually.
- Try right clicking on the mini and using “Replace Image” to insert another mini illustration from

#### Data, tables, and timelines
- To edit a chart, select it and click the dropdown arrow in the top right. Select “Open source.” The legend will need to be updated manually.
- For all charts, remember to not only use color alone to visualize and distinguish data. Utilize text sizing, spacing, contrast, and more. Please refer to
- Visual Accessibility at Red Hat
- To edit a chart, select it and click the dropdown arrow in the top right. Select “Open source.” The percentage will need to be updated manually.
- To edit a chart, select it and click the dropdown arrow in the top right. Select “Open source.”
- Try right clicking on the icon and using “Replace Image” to insert another icon from the

## 6. Placeholder Constraints (from template)

#### Getting started
- color, do not use red to represent negative things

#### Title and closing slides
- Optional subheading
- Presenter’s name
- title should not exceed two lines
- Presentation title should not exceed two lines
- Title should not exceed two lines
- Only use this title slide format when creating a webinar that must be viewed at a small size.

#### Dividers
- Optional section marker
- limit this slide to

#### Overview, agenda, content, and quotes
- Optional section marker
- Slide title should not exceed one line
- Optional subheading
- subheading should not exceed three lines lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetuer
- Optional section marker here
- Slide title should not exceed three lines. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetuer
- Optional subheading should not exceed three lines lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetuer

#### Data, tables, and timelines
- Optional section marker
- Slide title should not exceed one line
- Optional subheading
- Column header
- two lines maximum
- Slide title should not exceed three lines. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetuer
- Optional subheading should not exceed three lines Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetuer

## 7. Asset Catalogue

Extracted from the template PPTX:
- **Logos**: 4 files
- **Icons**: 33 files
- **Photos** (replaceable): 21 files
- **Charts**: 0 chart templates

Full manifest: `assets/manifest.yaml`

### Logos (FIXED — embed as-is)
- `assets/logos/8d658f962676.png`  1226×290px  → slides [1]
- `assets/logos/f3ec043eb316.png`  496×117px  → slide layout / master
- `assets/logos/47c91d6a9ffd.png`  306×72px  → slide layout / master
- `assets/logos/c7ffbc0e4954.png`  875×211px  → slides [9, 11, 62]

### Backgrounds (FIXED — apply to slide background fill)
- `assets/backgrounds/9e02dd953ab1.png`  2500×1407px
- `assets/backgrounds/28e57e9b9a1b.png`  2500×1407px
- `assets/backgrounds/db446344de7f.png`  2500×1407px
- `assets/backgrounds/00ae301f2339.png`  2500×1407px

### Icons — Layout (FIXED, 12 files)
  Used in slide layouts; do not replace.

### Icons — Content (REPLACEABLE, 21 files)
  Replace with appropriate icon from Red Hat icon library.
  Slides where icons appear: [1, 2, 3, 4, 8, 9, 11, 13, 14, 15]

### Asset Usage Rules
- **FIXED** assets → embed directly at the position recorded in `manifest.yaml`
- **REPLACEABLE** assets → record as placeholder; agent must substitute real content
- Logo always appears at the position recorded in `usages[source=layout]`
- Photos: use `python-pptx add_picture()` with the `w_pct × slide_width` and `h_pct × slide_height` values from the manifest

---

## 8. Accessibility

- Do **not** use colour alone to distinguish data series in charts.
- Add text labels, patterns, or icons alongside colour coding.
- Minimum body text size: 12pt on any slide.
- Ensure title text is at least 22pt.
- Follow [Red Hat Visual Accessibility Guidelines](https://brand.redhat.com/applications/presentations/).

---

## 9. Confidentiality Designators

Set the correct designator in **Slide → Edit Theme** before sharing:

| Audience | Designator |
|----------|-----------|
| External (public) | *(none)* |
| NDA partners | `Confidential: Red Hat associate and NDA partner use only` |
| Internal only | `Confidential: Red Hat associates only` |
| No forwarding | `Confidential: Red Hat associates only, No further distribution` |

---

## 10. Section Skill Files

Detailed narrative patterns for each section are in `sections/`:

| Section | File |
|---------|------|
| Title and Closing | `sections/title-closing.yaml` |
| Section Dividers | `sections/dividers.yaml` |
| Overview / Agenda / Content | `sections/overview-agenda.yaml` |
| Data, Tables, Timelines | `sections/data-tables-timelines.yaml` |
